title: On Mrs. Montagueï؟½s Feather-Hangings.
content:
آ آ آ آ The birds put off their every hue
آ آ آ آ To dress a room for Montagu.
آ آ آ آ The peacock sends his heavenly dyes,
آ آ آ آ His rainbows and his starry eyes;
آ آ آ آ The pheasant plumes, which round enfold
آ آ آ آ His mantling neck with downy gold;
آ آ آ آ The cock his archï؟½d tailï؟½s azure show;
آ آ آ آ And, river-blanchï؟½d, the swan his snow.
آ آ آ آ All tribes beside of Indian name,
آ آ آ آ That glossy shine, or vivid flame,
آ آ آ آ Where rises, and where sets the day,
آ آ آ آ Whateï؟½er they boast of rich and gay,
آ آ آ آ Contribute to the gorgeous plan,
آ آ آ آ Proud to advance it all they can.
آ آ آ آ This plumage neither dashing shower,
آ آ آ آ Nor blasts, that shake the dripping bower,
آ آ آ آ Shall drench again or discompose,
آ آ آ آ But, screenï؟½d from every storm that blows,
آ آ آ آ It boasts a splendour ever new,
آ آ آ آ Safe with protecting Montagu.
آ آ آ آ To the same patroness resort,
آ آ آ آ Secure of favour at her court,
آ آ آ آ Strong Genius, from whose forge of thought
آ آ آ آ Forms rise, to quick perfection wrought,
آ آ آ آ Which, though new-born, with vigour move,
آ آ آ آ Like Pallas springing armï؟½d from Joveï؟½
آ آ آ آ Imagination scattering round
آ آ آ آ Wild roses over furrowï؟½d ground,
آ آ آ آ Which Labour of his frown beguile,
آ آ آ آ And teach Philosophy a smileï؟½
آ آ آ آ Wit flashing on Religionï؟½s side,
آ آ آ آ Whose fires, to sacred truth applied,
آ آ آ آ The gem, though luminous before,
آ آ آ آ Obtrude on human notice more,
آ آ آ آ Like sunbeams on the golden height
آ آ آ آ Of some tall temple playing brightï؟½
آ آ آ آ Well tutorï؟½d Learning, from his books
آ آ آ آ Dismissï؟½d with grave, not haughty, looks,
آ آ آ آ Their order on his shelves exact,
آ آ آ آ Not more harmonious or compact
آ آ آ آ Than that to which he keeps confined
آ آ آ آ The various treasures of his mindï؟½
آ آ آ آ All these to Montaguï؟½s repair,
آ آ آ آ Ambitious of a shelter there.
آ آ آ آ There Genius, Learning, Fancy, Wit,
آ آ آ آ Their ruffled plumage calm refit
آ آ آ آ (For stormy troubles loudest roar
آ آ آ آ Around their flight who highest soar),
آ آ آ آ And in her eye, and by her aid,
آ آ آ آ Shine safe without a fear to fade.
آ آ آ آ She thus maintains divided sway
آ آ آ آ With yon bright regent of the day;
آ آ آ آ The Plume and Poet both we know
آ آ آ آ Their lustre to his influence owe;
آ آ آ آ And she the works of Phï؟½bus aiding,
آ آ آ آ Both Poet saves and Plume from fading.
meaning:
"On Mrs. Montagueï؟½s Feather-Hangings." is a quintessential example of William Cowper's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
